This plant is classified within the genus Crossoglossa, which is a group of orchids that are native to tropical regions. intro: Crossoglossa topoensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae, is a rare and endangered epiphyte native to the cloud forests of Ecuador. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and inflammation, due to its potential antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crossoglossa topoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
